steps.title: Separate the two clauses
steps.content: The sentence says Balos Beach sand is a more unusual shade, and then explains the reason: crushed coral and other organic matter make the sand pink. The word “though” belongs with the first clause, creating a contrast with most sand being beige.

steps.title: Eliminate punctuation problems
steps.content: Choice A joins two independent clauses with only a comma. Choice B puts “though” after a colon, which does not fit the contrast. Choice C leaves “though” before a comma but still creates a comma splice before the second independent clause. The semicolon correctly separates the clauses after “though.” The answer is D.
